member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.Assert.That
member.summary: Gets the singleton instance of the Assert functionality.
member.remarks: Users can use this to plug-in custom assertions through C# extension methods. For instance, the signature of a custom assertion provider could be "public static void IsOfType<T>(this Assert assert, object obj)" Users could then use a syntax similar to the default assertions which in this case is "Assert.That.IsOfType<Dog>(animal);" More documentation is at "https://github.com/Microsoft/testfx/docs/README.md".

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.AssertExtensions
member.summary: Provides That extension to Assert class.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.AssertExtensions.CompilerGeneratedDisplayClassRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
[A-Za-z0-9_\\.]+\\+<>c__DisplayClass\\d+_\\d+\\.(\\w+(?:\\.\\w+)*(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?)
Explanation:
○ Match a character in the set [.0-9A-Z_a-z] atomically at least once.
○ Match the string "+<>c__DisplayClass".
○ Match a Unicode digit atomically at least once.
○ Match '_'.
○ Match a Unicode digit atomically at least once.
○ Match '.'.
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match a word character greedily at least once.
○ Loop greedily any number of times.
○ Match '.'.
○ Match a word character greedily at least once.
○ Optional (greedy).
○ Match '['.
○ Match a character other than ']' atomically at least once.
○ Match ']'.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.CollectionAssert
member.summary: A collection of helper classes to test various conditions associated with collections within unit tests. If the condition being tested is not met, an exception is thrown.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.CollectionAssert.That
member.summary: Gets the singleton instance of the CollectionAssert functionality.
member.remarks: Users can use this to plug-in custom assertions through C# extension methods. For instance, the signature of a custom assertion provider could be "public static void AreEqualUnordered(this CollectionAssert customAssert, ICollection expected, ICollection actual)" Users could then use a syntax similar to the default assertions which in this case is "CollectionAssert.That.AreEqualUnordered(list1, list2);" More documentation is at "https://github.com/Microsoft/testfx/docs/README.md".

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.CollectionAssert.FindMismatchedElement``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.Collections.Generic.IEqualityComparer{``0},System.Int32@,System.Int32@,System.Object@)
member.summary: Finds a mismatched element between the two collections. A mismatched element is one that appears a different number of times in the expected collection than it does in the actual collection. The collections are assumed to be different non-null references with the same number of elements. The caller is responsible for this level of verification. If there is no mismatched element, the function returns false and the out parameters should not be used.
member.param: The first collection to compare.
The second collection to compare.
The equality comparer to use when comparing items.
The expected number of occurrences of or 0 if there is no mismatched element.
The actual number of occurrences of or 0 if there is no mismatched element.
The mismatched element (may be null) or null if there is no mismatched element.
member.returns: true if a mismatched element was found; false otherwise.

member.summary: compares the objects using object.Equals.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.CollectionAssert.Equals(System.Object,System.Object)
member.summary: Static equals overloads are used for comparing instances of two types for equality. This method should not be used for comparison of two instances for equality. Please use CollectionAssert.AreEqual and associated overloads in your unit tests.
member.param: Object A.
Object B.
member.returns: Never returns.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.CollectionAssert.ReferenceEquals(System.Object,System.Object)
member.summary: Static ReferenceEquals overloads are used for comparing instances of two types for reference equality. This method should not be used for comparison of two instances for reference equality. Please use CollectionAssert methods or Assert.AreSame and associated overloads in your unit tests.
member.param: Object A.
Object B.
member.returns: Never returns.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DataSourceAttribute
member.summary: Specifies connection string, table name and row access method for data driven testing.

item.description: This works only on .NET Framework and is not supported on .NET Core or later.

The following shows example usages for this attribute:
[DataSource("Provider=SQLOLEDB.1;Data Source=source;Integrated Security=SSPI;Initial Catalog=EqtCoverage;Persist Security Info=False", "MyTable")] [DataSource("dataSourceNameFromConfigFile")].

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DiscoverInternalsAttribute
member.summary: The presence of this attribute in a test assembly causes MSTest to discover test classes (i.e. classes having the "TestClass" attribute) and test methods (i.e. methods having the "TestMethod" attribute) which are declared internal in addition to test classes and test methods which are declared public. When this attribute is not present in a test assembly the tests in such classes will not be discovered.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.GlobalTestInitializeAttribute
member.summary: A global test initialize attribute that applies to every test method in the assembly. The method to which this attribute is applied must be public, static, non-generic, has a single parameter of type TestContext, and either returns void or a Task.
member.remarks: Multiple methods with this attribute in the assembly is allowed, but there is no guarantee of the order in which they will be executed. In addition, TimeoutAttribute isn't supported on methods with this attribute.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestInitializeAttribute
member.summary: The test initialize attribute.

member.name: F:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ParallelizeAttribute.DefaultExecutionScope
member.summary: The default scope for the parallel run. Although method level gives maximum parallelization, the default is set to class level to enable maximum number of customers to easily convert their tests to run in parallel. In most cases within a class tests aren't thread safe.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ParallelizeAttribute.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ParallelizeAttribute.Workers
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of workers to be used for the parallel run.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ParallelizeAttribute.Scope
member.summary: Gets or sets the scope of the parallel run.
member.remarks: To enable all classes to run in parallel set this to . To get the maximum parallelization level set this to .

member.summary: This attribute is used to conditionally control whether a test class or a test method will run or be ignored based on whether the test is running in a CI environment.
member.remarks: This attribute isn't inherited. Applying it to a base class will not affect derived classes.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: Decides whether the test should be included or excluded in CI environments.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.CIConditionAttribute.GroupName
member.summary: Gets the group name for this attribute.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ConditionBaseAttribute
member.summary: This attribute is used to conditionally control whether a test class or a test method will run or be ignored, based on a condition and using an optional message.
member.remarks: This attribute isn't inherited. Applying it to a base class will not affect derived classes.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ConditionBaseAttribute.#ctor(Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ConditionMode)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The condition mode.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ConditionBaseAttribute.Mode
member.summary: Gets the condition mode.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ConditionBaseAttribute.IgnoreMessage
member.summary: Gets or sets the ignore message indicating the reason for ignoring the test method or test class.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ConditionBaseAttribute.GroupName
member.summary: Gets the group name for this attribute. This is relevant when multiple attributes that inherit are present. The values of attributes in the same group are "OR"ed together. While the value from different groups is "AND"ed together. In other words, a test will be ignored if any group has all its values as false.
member.remarks: Usually, you can use to return the group name.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ConditionBaseAttribute.IsConditionMet
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the condition is met. The implementation of this property shouldn't read the property.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ConditionMode
member.summary: An enumeration used with to control whether the condition is reversed.

member.name: F:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ConditionMode.Include
member.summary: Runs only when the condition is met (the default).

member.name: F:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ConditionMode.Exclude
member.summary: Ignores the test when the condition is met (i.e, reverse the condition).

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DelayBackoffType
member.summary: Specifies a backoff type for the delay between retries.

member.name: F:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DelayBackoffType.Constant
member.summary: Specifies a constant backoff type. Meaning the delay between retries is constant.

member.name: F:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DelayBackoffType.Exponential
member.summary: Specifies an exponential backoff type. The delay is calculated as the base delay * 2^(n-1) where n is the retry attempt. For example, if the base delay is 1000ms, the delays will be 1000ms, 2000ms, 4000ms, 8000ms, etc.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class for the GitHub WorkItem Attribute.
member.param: The URL to a GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.GitHubWorkItemAttribute.Url
member.summary: Gets the URL to the GitHub issue associated.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.GitHubWorkItemAttribute.ExtractId(System.String)
member.summary: Extracts the ID from the GitHub issue/pull/discussion URL.
member.param: The URL to a GitHub ticket.
member.returns: The ticket ID.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.GitHubWorkItemAttribute.GitHubTicketRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
https:\\/\\/github\\.com\\/.+\\/.+\\/(issues|pull|discussions)\\/(\\d+)(#.+)?
Explanation:
○ Match the string "https://github.com/".
○ Match a character other than '\n' greedily at least once.
○ Match '/'.
○ Match a character other than '\n' greedily at least once.
○ Match '/'.
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match with 3 alternative expressions.
○ Match the string "issues".
○ Match the string "pull".
○ Match the string "discussions".
○ Match '/'.
○ 2nd capture group.
○ Match a Unicode digit greedily at least once.
○ Optional (greedy).
○ 3rd capture group.
○ Match '#'.
○ Match a character other than '\n' atomically at least once.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.IgnoreAttribute
member.summary: This attribute is used to ignore a test class or a test method, with an optional message.
member.remarks: This attribute isn't inherited. Applying it to a base class will not cause derived classes to be ignored.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.IgnoreAttribute.#ctor(System.String)

member.summary: This attribute is used to set a retry count on a test method in case of failure.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.RetryAttribute.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the given number of max retries.
member.param: The maximum number of retry attempts. This must be greater than or equal to 1.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.RetryAttribute.MaxRetryAttempts
member.summary: Gets the number of retries that the test should make in case of failures. Note that before RetryAttribute is considered, the test was already executed once. This property determines the max number of retries after the first normal run.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.RetryAttribute.MillisecondsDelayBetweenRetries
member.summary: Gets or sets the delay, in milliseconds, between retries. This delay is also applied after the first run and before the first retry attempt.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.RetryAttribute.BackoffType
member.summary: Gets or sets the delay backoff type.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.RetryAttribute.ExecuteAsync(Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.RetryContext)
member.summary: Retries the test method times in case of failure. Note that a first run of the method was already executed and failed before this method is called.
member.param: An object to encapsulate the state needed for retry execution.
member.returns: Returns a object that contains the results of all attempts. Only the last added element is used to determine the test outcome. The other results are currently not used, but may be used in the future for tooling to show the state of the failed attempts.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.RetryBaseAttribute
member.summary: An abstract attribute that controls retrying a test method if it failed. It's up to the derived classes to define how the retry is done.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.RetryBaseAttribute.ExecuteAsync(Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.RetryContext)
member.summary: Retries the test method. The details of how retry is done is left to the derived classes. Note that a first run of the method was already executed and failed before this method is called.
member.param: An object to encapsulate the state needed for retry execution.
member.returns: Returns a object that contains the results of all attempts. Only the last added element is used to determine the test outcome. The other results are currently not used, but may be used in the future for tooling to show the state of the failed attempts.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestCategoryBaseAttribute
member.summary: Base class for the "Category" attribute.
member.remarks: The reason for this attribute is to let the users create their own implementation of test categories. - test framework (discovery, etc) deals with TestCategoryBaseAttribute. - The reason that TestCategories property is a collection rather than a string, is to give more flexibility to the user. For instance the implementation may be based on enums for which the values can be OR'ed in which case it makes sense to have single attribute rather than multiple ones on the same test.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestCategoryBaseAttribute.#ctor

member.summary: Timeout attribute; used to specify the timeout of a unit test.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TimeoutAttribute.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The timeout in milliseconds.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TimeoutAttribute.Timeout
member.summary: Gets the timeout in milliseconds.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TimeoutAttribute.CooperativeCancellation
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the test method should be cooperatively canceled on timeout. When set to , the cancellation token is canceled on timeout, and the method completion is awaited. The test method and all the code it calls, must be designed in a way that it observes the cancellation and cancels cooperatively. If the test method does not complete, the timeout does not force it to complete. When set to , the cancellation token is canceled on timeout, timeout result is reported and the method task will continue running on background. This may lead to conflicts in file access on test cleanup, unobserved exceptions, and memory leaks.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TimeoutAttribute.HasCorrectTimeout
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the instance has the correct test timeout signature.
